data.body: PASS-1 review (criterion T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification): CLEAN, no findings. T1 bright-lines fire (novel architectural pattern + external-tooling integrations + LLM/agent design), and the plan satisfies all three T1 checks: (a) prior art is considered before committing — the design is explicitly the inverse of the existing completion-verifier, adopts the shared three-pass framework (epic 9da1), and is grounded in ~30 research reports + named techniques (Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE, RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK; external-grounded>self-reflection, anti-sycophancy, lost-in-the-middle); (b) novelty is justified vs established approaches (the 'hard-gate-is-anti-pattern' research was examined and found not to transfer to autonomous agents; three-pass replaces model-emitted severity with explicit reasoning); (c) capability assertions are resolved — the consumed grounding oracle / workflow engine / prompt storage are CLOSED prerequisite epics named with concrete module paths (src/rebar/grounding/, src/rebar/llm/workflow/, src/rebar/llm/prompts.py), not unverified 'library supports X' claims.
